children.name: Gospatric de Workington of High Ireby

km.Staffordshire, England: ស្តាហ្វូរដសហីរេ, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Orme De Workington Baron of Kendal於大約1090年出生在英國斯塔福爾。他和Gunnilda of Dunbar至少有1個兒子。他去世於1130年之後。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Orme De Workington Baron of Kendal于大约1090年出生在英国斯塔福爾。他和Gunnilda of Dunbar至少有1个儿子。他去世于1130年之后。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Workington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Worcestershire): habitational name from Wormington in Gloucestershire or Berry Wormington in Stanton (also Gloucestershire). Perhaps also from Warmington in Northamptonshire. The placenames derive from an Old English personal name Wyrma + Old English connective -ing- + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Kendal
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northwestern): variant of   Kendall  .

variants.name: Kendall
meanings: English: habitational name from Kendal in Cumbria, which takes its name from the river Kent + Old Norse dalr ‘valley’, or from the valley of the river Kent itself.

English (of Welsh origin): from an Anglicized form of the Welsh personal name Cynddelw, which was borne by a famous 12th-century Welsh poet. It probably derives from a Celtic word meaning ‘exalted, high’ + delw ‘image, effigy’.

nameMeanings.name: Orme
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Midlands): from the Middle English personal name Orm (Old Norse Ormr, from ormr ‘snake’).

variants.name: Ormes
meanings: English: variant of Orme , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Oram
meanings: Biblical name, meaning ‘height’ in Hebrew, borne by a son of Shem and grandson of Noah mentioned in a genealogy (Genesis 10: 22).

variants.name: Orem
meanings: Biblical name, apparently meaning ‘pine tree’ in Hebrew, mentioned in a genealogy (1 Chronicles 2:25). This name is in use in a number of different spellings. There may have been some confusion with the Irish name Oran , of which this is sometimes a variant spelling.

variants.name: Ormiston
meanings: Scottish (southeastScotland): habitational name from any of the places called Ormiston, in the former counties of Roxburghshire and East Lothian, named with the genitive case of the Old Norse personal name Ormr (see Orme ) + Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Orum
meanings: Danish (Ørum): habitational name from any of several places called Ørum, a compound of ør ‘gravel beach’ + hem ‘dwelling’. This surname is also found in Norway, where it is of Danish origin.
English (Wiltshire): variant of   Orme   and   Oram  .

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Orme De Workington Baron of Kendal was born about 1090, in Staffordshire, England. He had at least 1 son with Gunnilda of Dunbar. He died after 1130.

fr.lifeSummary: Orme De Workington Baron of Kendal est né environ 1090, à Staffordshire, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Gunnilda of Dunbar. Il est décédé après 1130.

de.lifeSummary: Orme De Workington Baron of Kendal wurde um 1090 in Staffordshire, England geboren. Er hatte mit Gunnilda of Dunbar mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb nach 1130.

it.lifeSummary: Orme De Workington Baron of Kendal è nato il circa 1090 nel Staffordshire, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Gunnilda of Dunbar. Morì dopo il 1130.

ja.Staffordshire, England: イギリススタフォードシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Orme De Workington Baron of Kendalは約1090年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアで生まれた。彼はGunnilda of Dunbarとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1130年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Orme De Workington Baron of Kendal(은)는 대략 1090년 영국 스태퍼드실르에서 태어났다. 그는 Gunnilda of Dunbar(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1130년 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Orme De Workington Baron of Kendal nasceu aproximadamente 1090, em Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Gunnilda of Dunbar. Ele faleceu após 1130.

ru.lifeSummary: Orme De Workington Baron of Kendal родился около 1090 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Gunnilda of Dunbar. Он умер после 1130 г.
